_title: NFPA 70 Article 430 — Motors, Motor Circuits, and Controllers

articles.scope: Motor branch-circuit conductor sizing (430.22, 430.24), motor short-circuit/ground-fault protection (430.52), motor overload (430.32), motor controller (430.81), motor disconnect (430.102), motor control centres (430.94+).

key_sections.title: Ampacity and Motor Rating Determination
key_sections.summary: Use Tables 430.247 / 430.248 / 430.250 / 430.251 (full-load currents) for single-motor branch circuits; use nameplate for thermal overload

key_sections.title: Single Motor — Conductors
key_sections.summary: Branch-circuit conductors ≥ 125% × motor FLA from Table 430.250 (3-phase) or 430.248 (single-phase)

key_sections.title: Several Motors or Motors plus Other Loads
key_sections.summary: Conductors ≥ 125% × largest motor FLA + 100% × other motors' FLA + 100% × non-motor loads

key_sections.title: Continuous-Duty Motors — Overload Protection
key_sections.summary: Overload sized at 115% to 125% of motor nameplate FLA (Service Factor and motor type dependent)

key_sections.title: Motor Branch-Circuit Short-Circuit and Ground-Fault Protection
key_sections.summary: OCPD sized by Table 430.52(C)(1) percentage: typical 250% inverse-time breaker / 300% non-time-delay fuse / 175% dual-element time-delay fuse / 800% instantaneous-trip breaker (all of motor FLA from Table 430.250)

key_sections.title: Disconnecting Means
key_sections.summary: Disconnect within sight of the controller AND within sight of the motor (or lockable disconnect within sight of controller meets both)

Table_430.250_Full_Load_Current_3-phase_.title: Full-Load Current (A) — Three-Phase AC Motors at 460 V (selected)
Table_430.250_Full_Load_Current_3-phase_._note: For sizing branch-circuit conductors and OCPDs. Always use TABLE FLA (not nameplate FLA) for these calculations.

Table_430.52(C)(1)_Branch_Circuit_OCPD_P.title: Maximum Branch-Circuit OCPD as % of Motor FLA

common_errors: Using nameplate FLA for branch-circuit conductor sizing — must use Table 430.250 FLA (430.6(A)(1))
Using Table 430.250 FLA for overload sizing — must use nameplate FLA (430.6(A)(2))
Sizing OCPD at 100% of motor FLA — motor must be sized at 250% (inverse-time breaker) or 175% (dual-element fuse) to handle starting inrush
Disconnect not within sight of motor — 430.102(B) violation. 'Within sight' = visible from one location to the other AND ≤50 ft apart
related_iec_60364: IEC 60204-1 (Safety of machinery — electrical equipment of machines)
articles.divergence_notes: NEC Art 430 uses horsepower-based FLA tables (430.247-251) and a percentage-based OCPD sizing rule (430.52). IEC sizes motors per actual rated current with diversity factors and protective-device coordination per IEC 60364-5-53. NEC's 'within sight' disconnect rule (430.102) has no IEC equivalent — IEC requires accessible local isolation but does not specify sightline.
articles.usage_notes: Always cite TABLE FLA when sizing conductors/OCPDs and NAMEPLATE FLA when sizing overloads. Confusing the two is the #1 motor-circuit inspection failure.
